tr.td: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Amlodipine (as amlodipine besylate) 2.5 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (2.5 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Alendronic acid (as alendronate sodium) 10 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (81 mg) by mouth once daily
Atorvastatin (as atorvastatin calcium) 40 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (40 mg) by mouth once daily
Prasugrel (as prasugrel hydrochloride) 10 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth twice daily
1 tablet (5 mg) by mouth once daily
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Development of individualized plan of care
Assessment of health and social care needs
Depression screening using Patient Health Questionnaire Two-Item score (procedure)
Assessment using AUDIT-C (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Consumption)
Urinalysis with reflex to microscopy and culture
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction (disorder)
Assessment of cardiac status using monitoring device

ul.li: Patient will have scheduled follow-up with cardiology and primary care within 7 days post-discharge to monitor for complications related to aortic valve replacement.
All medications, including anticoagulation and heart failure therapies, will be reviewed and reconciled within 48 hours of discharge to prevent adverse drug events.
Assess renal function post-discharge due to history of chronic kidney disease and diabetes.
Maintain systolic blood pressure below 130 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Patient and caregivers will be educated prior to discharge on signs of heart failure, infection, and when to seek urgent care.
Patient will report improved sleep quality and reduced daytime sleepiness.
Reduce apnea-hypopnea index (AHI) to less than 5 events per hour.
Maintain nocturnal oxygen saturation above 90% during sleep.
Patient will use CPAP device for at least 4 hours per night on 70% of nights.
Reduce the severity of knee pain associated with osteoarthritis to improve mobility and quality of life.
Increase ability to perform activities of daily living without significant limitation from knee osteoarthritis.
Increase knee flexion range of motion to support ambulation and reduce stiffness.
Achieve and maintain LDL cholesterol below 70 mg/dL to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Maintain total cholesterol below 150 mg/dL.
Maintain triglycerides below 150 mg/dL.
Maintain HDL cholesterol above 40 mg/dL.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular and renal risk.
Adopt dietary changes (DASH diet, sodium restriction), regular physical activity, weight management, and reduced alcohol intake to support blood pressure control.

tr.td: Vaccine product containing only Influenza virus antigen (medicinal product)
Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Aspartate aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Home continuous positive airway pressure unit (physical object)

ul.li: Patient will have scheduled follow-up with cardiology and primary care within 7 days post-discharge to monitor for complications related to aortic valve replacement.
All medications, including anticoagulation and heart failure therapies, will be reviewed and reconciled within 48 hours of discharge to prevent adverse drug events.
Assess renal function post-discharge due to history of chronic kidney disease and diabetes.
Maintain systolic blood pressure below 130 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Patient and caregivers will be educated prior to discharge on signs of heart failure, infection, and when to seek urgent care.
Patient will report improved sleep quality and reduced daytime sleepiness.
Reduce apnea-hypopnea index (AHI) to less than 5 events per hour.
Maintain nocturnal oxygen saturation above 90% during sleep.
Patient will use CPAP device for at least 4 hours per night on 70% of nights.
Reduce the severity of knee pain associated with osteoarthritis to improve mobility and quality of life.
Increase ability to perform activities of daily living without significant limitation from knee osteoarthritis.
Increase knee flexion range of motion to support ambulation and reduce stiffness.
Achieve and maintain LDL cholesterol below 70 mg/dL to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Maintain total cholesterol below 150 mg/dL.
Maintain triglycerides below 150 mg/dL.
Maintain HDL cholesterol above 40 mg/dL.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular and renal risk.
Adopt dietary changes (DASH diet, sodium restriction), regular physical activity, weight management, and reduced alcohol intake to support blood pressure control.
